frameworks.name: leaky competing accumulator
frameworks.one_line: Neural accumulator network model of perceptual decision-making

frameworks.name: tuned normalization
frameworks.one_line: Heterogeneous normalization strengths across decision-circuit neurons

frameworks.name: Bayesian confidence as probability correct
frameworks.one_line: View that confidence reflects probability the decision is correct

frameworks.name: positive-evidence heuristic
frameworks.one_line: Confidence tracks magnitude of evidence for the chosen option

core_question: Can tuned normalization in decision circuits explain dissociations between confidence and decision accuracy?
key_assumptions: Confidence and choice can be supported by partly distinct neural populations
Normalization tuning varies systematically across decision-circuit neurons
A simple LCA architecture is sufficient to capture confidence behavior
manipulations: Varied each accumulator unit's normalization tuning
Compared model predictions against published confidence-accuracy dissociations
Tested heterogeneous normalization in macaque superior colliculus neurons
key_findings: LCA with tuned normalization reproduces counterintuitive confidence-accuracy dissociations
Each unit's normalization tuning dictates its weight for decisions versus confidence
Systematic variability in normalization tuning is observed in macaque superior colliculus
interpretations: Confidence reflects evidence magnitude for the decision rather than probability correct
Tuned normalization in decision circuitry is a biologically plausible substrate for this dissociation
